Off Calendar Mar-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: EMILY WICK, ESQ., 415.398.8308, FOR INTERVENOR YAN (VICKY) XIONG.
DAVID FRANCIS PERRY, ESQ., 415.699.4477, FOR PLAINTIFF THE NATIONAL COLLECTION AGENCY, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court VACATED its tentative ruling: INTERVENOR YAN (VICKY) XIONG's Motion To Quash Notice Of Levy And Or Recover IS GRANTED. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2011 Continued To Dec-02-11 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Default Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Oct-20-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Tomar Mason, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: B. Fields, CSR #7235

Fred Keeperman, Esq. appeared for plaintiff(s) The National Collection Agency, Inc., a Corp.
Plaintiff's Witness: Gerald Savela was placed under oath and direct examined.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff(s), and against the Defaulted Defendant(s) Michael Binningger and Steven Binninger for Judgment as follows:

Damages/Principal - $40,489.63
Prejudgment Interest - $2,473.07
Attorney's Fees - $2,825.00
Costs - $655.00
Total judgment in the amount of $46,442.70

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
